note: Curated public view of the research program, rendered as bold thread bands by bin/build_network.py. Hand-authored by Mao Li; not auto-generated from the private map. Unpublished work is summarized as an 'N in progress' chip so momentum shows without disclosing results.
question: Can language models stand in for people in social measurement?

threads.name: Digital twins of respondents
threads.blurb: Can a persona-conditioned model reproduce a specific person's survey answers? Where it succeeds, it is a measurement tool; where it fails, the failure itself is the finding.

wip.title: Opinion change in twins
wip.desc: Do persona twins shift their views like the people they model?

wip.title: What conditioning encodes
wip.desc: Probing which persona attributes the model actually represents internally.

wip.title: Detecting synthetic respondents
wip.desc: Telling model-generated survey answers apart from human ones.

threads.name: Validity of social simulation
threads.blurb: The same question at population scale: do societies of language-model agents reproduce social processes, or only their outcomes? Outcome fidelity can conceal process failure.

papers.title: Persona-Based Simulation of Human Opinion at Population Scale

threads.name: Who social media speaks for
threads.blurb: My dissertation. Inferring demographics and stance from social-media text, and asking whom that expression actually represents.

threads.name: LLMs as survey instruments
threads.blurb: Turning the method into tools: AI interviewers and model-assisted coding of open-ended responses, built and evaluated as survey instruments in their own right.

wip.title: Evaluating AI interviewers
wip.desc: A testbed and metrics for machine-administered survey interviews.

wip.title: LLM-assisted response coding
wip.desc: Model coding of open-ended answers, validated against human coders.

threads.name: Structuring text at scale
threads.blurb: The infrastructure the other threads lean on: efficient ranking and embedding-space summarization for large corpora of posts.
